steps.title: Use the repeated output
steps.content: The table has $f(22)=-10$ and $f(28)=-10$. A factored quadratic that gives the same output at $x=22$ and $x=28$ is built from $(x-22)(x-28)$, because that product is $0$ at both inputs. To make the output $-10$ at those inputs, use $$f(x)=(x-22)(x-28)-10.$$

steps.title: Use the equal outputs as a quick check
steps.content: The table also shows $f(22)=-10$ and $f(28)=-10$. In $f(x)=(x-22)(x-28)-10$, plugging in $x=22$ or $x=28$ makes the product 0, so both values are $-10$. Then $f(30)=(8)(2)-10=6$, matching the third row.

steps.title: Eliminate the other choices
steps.content:
• A: $f(x)=(x-22)(x-28)+5$ gives $f(22)=5 eq-10$. Eliminated.
  • B: $f(x)=(x-22)(x-28)-10$ gives $f(22)=-10$, $f(28)=-10$, $f(30)=6$. All match.
  • C: $f(x)=(x-10)(x-30)+30$ gives $f(30)=30 eq6$. Eliminated.
  • D: $f(x)=(x-10)(x-30)-30$ gives $f(30)=-30 eq6$. Eliminated.
That is the correct answer.
